panel: ALL LOCAL SYNTHETIC CASES - 700 CURRENT CASES
panel_note: Seven internal 100-case groups, excluding the separate prior-regression stress bucket.
title: AI parsing failures across 700 stable synthetic cases
what_test_shows: Counts failed cases across the seven current synthetic categories.
why_important: This tells us whether ordinary-to-messy local cases are staying stable while we work on harder inputs.

panel: ALL LOCAL SYNTHETIC CASES - 700 CURRENT CASES
panel_note: Seven internal 100-case groups, excluding the separate prior-regression stress bucket.
title: Workflow passes across 700 stable synthetic cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases that passed the local workflow expectations.
why_important: This is the broad smoke signal that the prototype path still works on the main local data set.

panel: LIVE API / GPT EXTRACTION - 50 CASE SMOKE RUNS
panel_note: Saved live OpenAI API runs against the local API route. These are model-dependent GPT extraction checks, so read them separately from deterministic local test data.
title: GPT extraction failures in 50 live API cases
what_test_shows: Counts failed cases from saved live OpenAI API extraction runs.
why_important: This shows whether the real GPT-backed extraction path is moving in the same direction as local parser tests.

panel: LIVE API / GPT EXTRACTION - 50 CASE SMOKE RUNS
panel_note: Saved live OpenAI API runs against the local API route. These are model-dependent GPT extraction checks, so read them separately from deterministic local test data.
title: Tree-count/detail failures in 12 live GPT tree-detail cases
what_test_shows: Counts live GPT extraction failures in the tree-count and tree-detail category.
why_important: This is the closest live API check for messy tree count/detail interpretation.

panel: LIVE API / GPT EXTRACTION - 50 CASE SMOKE RUNS
panel_note: Saved live OpenAI API runs against the local API route. These are model-dependent GPT extraction checks, so read them separately from deterministic local test data.
title: Live API passed cases in 50 GPT extraction runs
what_test_shows: Counts saved live API cases that passed the smoke-test expectations.
why_important: This is the workflow-facing signal that GPT extraction still produces usable structured output.

panel: LIVE API / GPT EXTRACTION - 50 CASE SMOKE RUNS
panel_note: Saved live OpenAI API runs against the local API route. These are model-dependent GPT extraction checks, so read them separately from deterministic local test data.
title: Messy job description failures in 12 live GPT cases
what_test_shows: Counts live GPT failures on messy job-description extraction cases.
why_important: This checks whether real API extraction handles the kind of rough input text the app is built around.

panel: LIVE API / GPT EXTRACTION - 50 CASE SMOKE RUNS
panel_note: Saved live OpenAI API runs against the local API route. These are model-dependent GPT extraction checks, so read them separately from deterministic local test data.
title: Local fallback/mock use in 50 live API cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases that did not use the live OpenAI path in saved API runs.
why_important: This confirms the API/GPT panel is based on live extraction rather than mock fallback behavior.

panel_note: A deliberately hard bucket. Some cases can produce more than one finding, so finding counts can exceed 100.
title: Price extraction findings in 100 prior-regression cases
what_test_shows: Counts missing or wrong price findings; a single case may have more than one price finding.
why_important: Prices are the worst current parser problem and directly affect estimate accuracy.

panel_note: A deliberately hard bucket. Some cases can produce more than one finding, so finding counts can exceed 100.
title: Tree-count mismatches in 100 prior-regression cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases where structured tree count did not match expected output.
why_important: This is where AI parsing should improve over time, even though TD2 can now resolve unclear counts.

panel_note: A deliberately hard bucket. Some cases can produce more than one finding, so finding counts can exceed 100.
title: Missing warnings in 100 prior-regression cases
what_test_shows: Counts expected warnings that were not shown.
why_important: Warnings let the contractor see uncertainty without blocking every estimate.

panel_note: A deliberately hard bucket. Some cases can produce more than one finding, so finding counts can exceed 100.
title: Missing follow-up in 100 prior-regression cases
what_test_shows: Counts expected clarification prompts that were not shown.
why_important: Follow-up is the handoff from messy AI interpretation to contractor-provided clarity.

panel_note: A deliberately hard bucket. Some cases can produce more than one finding, so finding counts can exceed 100.
title: Unexpected-ready in 100 prior-regression cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases where estimate creation was available before expected clarification.
why_important: This protects against the button appearing too early on truly unclear work.

panel_note: Legacy hard fixture used to measure strict parser quality separately from workflow recovery.
title: Strict parser/readiness failures in 150 hard cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict fixture mismatches before giving credit for TD2 resolution.
why_important: This remains a parser-improvement score, not the product workflow success score.

panel_note: Legacy hard fixture used to measure strict parser quality separately from workflow recovery.
title: Tree-count parser misses in 150 hard cases
what_test_shows: Counts hard cases where AI did not infer the expected tree count by itself.
why_important: This measures future AI improvement in converting confusing count language into structured data.

panel_note: Legacy hard fixture used to measure strict parser quality separately from workflow recovery.
title: Price-option parser misses in 150 hard cases
what_test_shows: Counts hard cases where option pricing did not match expectation.
why_important: Price parsing is high-risk because it affects the estimate amount.

panel_note: Legacy hard fixture used to measure strict parser quality separately from workflow recovery.
title: Recovered after follow-up in 150 hard cases
what_test_shows: Counts hard cases that became estimate-ready after clarification.
why_important: This is good: unclear AI interpretation is resolved through contractor/follow-up workflow.

panel_note: Legacy hard fixture used to measure strict parser quality separately from workflow recovery.
title: Still blocked after follow-up in 150 hard cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases still unable to reach estimate generation after simulated follow-up.
why_important: For the workflow, this is the key success measure: if it reaches estimate creation, the workflow worked.

panel_note: Uber-messy and uber-plus-messy cohorts. These are more important than easy/medium cases for measuring messy-input progress.
title: Recovered after follow-up in 300 uber messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts uber messy cases that required follow-up and then became ready.
why_important: This shows the clarification workflow is doing useful work instead of treating AI uncertainty as failure.

panel_note: Uber-messy and uber-plus-messy cohorts. These are more important than easy/medium cases for measuring messy-input progress.
title: Tree-count parser misses in 150 uber-messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts tree-count parser misses on the messiest alpha cohort.
why_important: This is a direct measure of whether AI is getting better at confusing count language.

panel_note: Uber-messy and uber-plus-messy cohorts. These are more important than easy/medium cases for measuring messy-input progress.
title: Strict failures in 150 uber-messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict fixture failures in the uber-messy cohort.
why_important: This is the parser-quality view before giving workflow credit for clarification.

panel_note: Uber-messy and uber-plus-messy cohorts. These are more important than easy/medium cases for measuring messy-input progress.
title: Strict failures in 150 uber-plus-messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict fixture failures in the extra messy plus cohort.
why_important: This confirms whether the broader messy parser coverage is staying stable.

panel_note: Uber-messy and uber-plus-messy cohorts. These are more important than easy/medium cases for measuring messy-input progress.
title: Still blocked after follow-up in 300 uber messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts uber messy cases still blocked after simulated clarification.
why_important: If this is zero, the workflow can reach estimate creation even when AI needed help.

panel_note: Easy, medium, medium-messy, and very-messy cohorts. Worst bars are placed first.
title: Recovered after follow-up in 600 other alpha cases
source: Alpha easy + medium + medium-messy + very-messy cohorts
what_test_shows: Counts non-uber cases that became ready after clarification.
why_important: This shows follow-up is being tracked separately from parser weakness.

panel_note: Easy, medium, medium-messy, and very-messy cohorts. Worst bars are placed first.
title: Strict failures in 150 very-messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict failures in very messy but not uber-messy inputs.
why_important: This checks whether lower-tier messy input remains under control.

panel_note: Easy, medium, medium-messy, and very-messy cohorts. Worst bars are placed first.
title: Strict failures in 150 medium-messy cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict failures in medium-messy inputs.
why_important: This should stay low while improvements target harder cases.

panel_note: Easy, medium, medium-messy, and very-messy cohorts. Worst bars are placed first.
title: Strict failures in 150 medium cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict failures in medium difficulty inputs.
why_important: Regression here would mean we broke normal parser behavior.

panel_note: Easy, medium, medium-messy, and very-messy cohorts. Worst bars are placed first.
title: Strict failures in 150 easy cases
what_test_shows: Counts strict failures in easy inputs.
why_important: Easy cases should remain clean while hard-case work continues.

panel_note: Easy, medium, medium-messy, and very-messy cohorts. Worst bars are placed first.
title: Still blocked after follow-up in 600 other alpha cases
source: Alpha easy + medium + medium-messy + very-messy cohorts
what_test_shows: Counts cases still blocked after simulated follow-up across non-uber cohorts.
why_important: This confirms normal and moderately messy workflows still reach estimate creation.

panel_note: Small hand-picked cases that catch behavior we have historically handled poorly.
title: Unexpected-ready failures in 14 focused cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases where the app allowed estimate creation before expected clarification.
why_important: This is the focused workflow guardrail against moving too fast on unclear inputs.

panel_note: Small hand-picked cases that catch behavior we have historically handled poorly.
title: Missing follow-up failures in 14 focused cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases where clarification text was expected but missing.
why_important: This keeps follow-up visible as its own workflow result.

panel_note: Small hand-picked cases that catch behavior we have historically handled poorly.
title: Misleading summary wording in 14 focused cases
what_test_shows: Counts focused cases where TD2 job summary wording changed the work meaning.
why_important: This measures conversion of messy input into clean, accurate structured work text.

panel_note: Small hand-picked cases that catch behavior we have historically handled poorly.
title: Uncertain prices finalized in 14 focused cases
what_test_shows: Counts cases where maybe/around prices still looked firm.
why_important: Uncertain prices must stay visible to the contractor instead of becoming customer estimate amounts.
